stimulus: Several outer planets in our solar system have ring systems, but the systems vary widely in how many main rings each planet has. According to the table, two planets that each have fewer than six main rings are ______
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the statement?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The table shows Jupiter has 4 main rings and Neptune has 5 main rings — both fewer than six.

Choice A is incorrect: Saturn has 7 and Uranus has 13. Choice C is incorrect: both Saturn (7) and Uranus (13) exceed six. Choice D is incorrect: while Neptune has 5, Saturn has 7.
